subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Evacuation Planning
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Evacuation Planning.

hook.text: Hurricane Katrina evacuation order: 24 hours before landfall. 100,000 people had no way to leave.

buildup.text: Evacuation planning includes routes, fuel, supplies, pet plans, and a meeting point for family.

discovery.text: Having two planned routes prevents getting trapped when the primary route is congested or blocked.

twist.text: Evacuating too late is often more dangerous than sheltering in place—roads become parking lots.

climax.text: Community evacuation plans that include transportation for the carless save the most vulnerable.

punchline.text: Two routes. Full tank. Go early. Planners leave; the rest get trapped.

quiz.question: Why should evacuation plans include two routes?
options: The primary route may be congested or blocked
